pages.name: Home
pages.description: Landing page for the cybersecurity / hacking blog with quick access to categories, search, featured content, and login entry for the single user.
primary_functions: Let the user see featured and latest articles, guides, reviews, and write-ups across all topics.
Provide a prominent search bar to start searches like VPN setup or password manager review.
Provide main navigation entry points into key categories such as Beginner, Threats, Tools, and CTF & Practice.
Surface links to popular series such as Web App Penetration Testing and other highlighted learning paths.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on "Beginner" in the main navigation header
Click on "Threats" in the main navigation header
Click on "Tools" in the main navigation header
Click on "CTF & Practice" in the main navigation header
Click on "Series" in the main navigation header
Submit the global search form with a query like "VPN setup" or "password manager review"
Click on a featured article card
Click on a latest article card
Click on a popular series card such as "Web App Penetration Testing"
Click on "Reading List" in the footer or user/profile menu
Click on "Settings" in the footer or user/profile menu
Click on "About" in the footer
Click on "Contact" in the footer
Click on "Privacy Policy" in the footer
Click on "Terms of Use" in the footer

access_methods.description: Accessed via the "Home" link in the main navigation
Default landing page at index.html

pages.name: Category Listing
pages.description: Reusable listing page for a single content category (Beginner, Threats, Tools, CTF & Practice) showing filterable and sortable article cards.
primary_functions: Display a list of articles in the chosen category (e.g., category.html?categoryId=beginner, category.html?categoryId=threats) using article cards.
Provide tag-based filtering (e.g., Wi_fi_security, phishing) via a sidebar tag section or tag filter area.
Provide additional filters such as date range (From/To), reading time limits, difficulty (e.g., Intermediate), and minimum upvotes.
Provide sorting controls (e.g., Newest first, Shortest reading time, Highest rated, Most commented) above the article list.
Allow opening an article by clicking its title to go to article.html?articleId=123.
Allow quick bookmarking from each article card via a bookmark icon, adding items to the default Reading List.

incoming_params.description: Category identifier (beginner, threats, tools, ctf_practice) determining which category listing to display.
Optional tag slug used to pre-filter the listing (e.g., wifi_security, phishing).
Optional ISO date string representing the earliest publication date to include.
Optional ISO date string representing the latest publication date to include.
Optional maximum reading time in minutes (e.g., 8 for beginner Wi-Fi security filter, 10 for phishing).
Optional difficulty filter, primarily for CTF & Practice (e.g., intermediate).
Optional minimum upvote count for listing articles (e.g., 5 or 10).
Optional minimum CTF challenge year (e.g., 2024) for write-ups.
Optional maximum CTF challenge year (e.g., 2024) for write-ups.
Sort mode applied to the listing (e.g., newest, shortest_reading_time, highest_rated, most_commented).
Current page of results for pagination.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on an article card title in the category listing
Click on a tag in the sidebar or tag chips to filter by tag (e.g., Wi-Fi Security, Phishing)
Change filters or sorting controls and apply them to refresh the listing
Use the global search bar in the header from a category page
Click on the user avatar/profile menu and select "Reading List"
Click on the site logo or "Home" in the header navigation

access_methods.description: Accessed via category links in the main navigation (Beginner, Threats, Tools, CTF & Practice)
category.html?categoryId=... plus optional filter parameters control which listing and filters are shown
Linked from article category labels or tag links

pages.name: Search Results
pages.description: Search results page where users see and refine results for text queries using content type and topic filters.
primary_functions: Show results for a search query (e.g., search.html?query=vpn_setup or search.html?query=password_manager_review) as article cards.
Filter results by content type (e.g., Guides / How_tos, Reviews, Articles, Write_ups, Series_parts).
Filter results by topic tags from a sidebar (e.g., tools, account_security, wifi_security) with the ability to select multiple topics.
Offer sorting options such as Relevance, Newest, Most_commented, and Highest_rated above the results list.
Allow opening results in new tabs (e.g., Ctrl+click on card titles) leading to article.html pages for deeper comparison.
Allow quick bookmarking of results to the default Reading List from the search results list.

incoming_params.description: Full-text search query string (e.g., "VPN setup", "password manager review").
Comma-separated list of content type values to include (e.g., guides_how_tos,reviews).
Comma-separated list of topic tag slugs selected in the sidebar (e.g., tools,account_security).
Sort mode for search results (e.g., relevance, newest, most_commented, highest_rated).
Current results page for pagination.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Adjust content type, topic tag filters, or sort dropdown and apply filters
Click on a pagination control to move to another page of results
Click (or Ctrl+click) on an article title in the search results list
Click on a category label inside a search result card
Use the header/user menu to open the Reading List after bookmarking results

access_methods.description: Submitting the global search form navigates to search.html with a query parameter
search.html?query=... plus optional filter parameters controls which search results are shown

pages.name: Article Detail
pages.description: Detail page for individual content pieces (tutorials, guides, reviews, write-ups, series parts) including comments, feedback, collections, and sharing tools.
primary_functions: Display full article content with metadata such as title, category, tags, published date, reading time, and upvotes.
Allow bookmarking via a bookmark icon to save the article into the default Reading List.
Provide an Add to collection or advanced bookmark button that opens a dialog to choose or create collections (e.g., Practice Later, Research, Weekend Practice).
Show and allow changing a reading status dropdown (e.g., Not_started, In_progress, Completed) directly on the article.
Display a comments section with visible total comment count and a comment form to post new comments.
Provide a share toolbar near the title with a Copy_link action that copies the article URL to the clipboard.
Provide a feedback section with star rating (including selecting 4 stars), multiple-choice reasons, and an optional text comment field, and allow submitting feedback.
For review articles (e.g., password manager reviews), clearly present recommended tools lists so users can count distinct tool names.
For CTF write-ups, present challenge metadata (year, difficulty, upvotes) and detailed steps for practice.
For series parts (e.g., Web App Penetration Testing Part 1), link back to the series overview and include sections such as Prerequisites.

incoming_params.description: Identifier of the article to display in detail.
Optional human-readable slug used in URLs alongside articleId for SEO-friendly links.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on the article's category label (e.g., Beginner, Threats, Tools, CTF & Practice)
Click on the series name/breadcrumb on a series part (e.g., Web App Penetration Testing)
Open the Reading List from the header/profile menu after bookmarking or adding to collections
Click "View collection" from the Add to collection dialog for a specific collection
Use the global search bar while viewing an article

access_methods.description: Accessed as article.html?articleId=... (optionally with &slug=...)
Linked from article cards in listings, search results, series parts lists, and collections

pages.name: Series List
pages.description: Overview page listing all multi-part learning series with topic filtering and popularity sorting.
primary_functions: Display a grid or list of all series with titles, brief descriptions, topics, and popularity indicators.
Provide a topic filter (e.g., web_application_security, network_security, osint) to narrow down series by area.
Provide sorting options such as Most_popular and Newest to order the series list.
Allow opening a specific series overview page (e.g., series_detail.html?seriesId=web_app_penetration_testing) by clicking a series card.

incoming_params.description: Optional topic filter for the series (e.g., web_application_security, network_security, osint, general).
Sort mode for series (e.g., most_popular, newest).
Current page in the series list for pagination.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a series card, such as "Web App Penetration Testing"
Change topic filter or sorting on the Series List page
Click a call-to-action like "Start Part 1" directly from a series card (if present)

access_methods.description: Accessed via the "Series" link in the main navigation bar
Optional topic and sort parameters control visible series

pages.name: Series Overview
pages.description: Table-of-contents page for a specific series, including metadata and the ordered list of parts.
primary_functions: Show high-level information about the series, including description, topic, total parts, and estimated total time.
List all parts of the series in order (Part 1, Part 2, Part 3, Part 4, etc.) with titles and reading times.
Allow opening any part in the series (e.g., Part 14) in the current tab or a new tab, linking to article.html?articleId=456 for each part.
Highlight important sections such as prerequisites or recommended reading so users can easily find where to begin.

incoming_params.description: Identifier of the series to display (e.g., the Web App Penetration Testing series).

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click or Ctrl+click on a series part title (e.g., Parts 1–4) in the parts list
Click on a breadcrumb or back link to return to the Series List

access_methods.description: Accessed as series_detail.html?seriesId=...
Linked from series cards on the Series List and from series-part articles

pages.name: Reading List & Collections
pages.description: Personal library view of the default Reading List and any user-defined collections such as Practice Later, Research, or Weekend Practice.
primary_functions: Display the default Reading List containing all articles saved via the bookmark icon from listings or article pages.
Allow switching between different collections (e.g., Practice Later, Research, Weekend Practice) in a tabbed or sidebar interface.
List saved articles in each collection with key metadata (title, category, reading time, date saved) and links to open the full article on article.html.
Allow removing articles from the Reading List or a collection, or adjusting their reading status from this overview.
Provide lightweight management for collections such as renaming or deleting collections, if needed, to keep saved items organized.

incoming_params.description: Optional collection identifier indicating which collection should be shown as active; defaults to the default Reading List when omitted.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a saved article title in any collection or the Reading List
Switch to another collection via the sidebar or tabs
Click on the site logo or "Home" in the header while on the Reading List page

access_methods.description: Accessed via the "Reading List" link in the site footer
Accessed via a user/profile menu item labeled "Reading List"
Optional collectionId parameter controls which collection is selected

pages.name: Settings
pages.description: Single-user settings hub, focusing on per-topic notification configuration like OSINT and Malware.
primary_functions: Provide a settings layout with a sidebar or tab navigation including a Notifications section.
In the Notifications section, display a topic-based table (e.g., OSINT, Malware, wifi_security, ctf_practice) with controls for each row.
Allow setting notification type (e.g., On_site_alerts, Email, None) and frequency (e.g., Instant, Daily, Weekly) for individual topics such as OSINT.
Allow disabling notifications for specific topics such as Malware by selecting a Disabled or Off option.
Provide a Save_changes button at the bottom of the settings page to apply and persist notification preferences.

incoming_params.description: Optional tab identifier (e.g., notifications) to deep-link directly to a specific settings section.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on the "Notifications" option in the settings sidebar
Click on the site logo or "Home" in the header while in Settings

access_methods.description: Accessed via the "Settings" link in the site footer
Accessed via a user/profile menu entry labeled "Settings"
Optional tab parameter selects the active settings section

pages.name: About
pages.description: Background page about the blog author, their cybersecurity focus, and responsible usage of hacking content.
primary_functions: Explain who runs the blog, their experience in cybersecurity, and the goals of the content.
Clarify the intended audience (e.g., beginners, intermediate practitioners) and how to use the material responsibly.
Provide any security/legal disclaimers specific to hacking and penetration testing content.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a contact link or call-to-action in the About content
Click on the site logo or "Home" in the header while viewing About

access_methods.description: Accessed via the "About" link in the site footer
Linked from other pages when explaining the author or site mission

pages.name: Contact
pages.description: Contact and feedback page where the user can see contact methods and submit a feedback form.
primary_functions: Provide contact details such as an email address or secure contact methods.
Offer a simple contact or feedback form allowing the user to send questions, bug reports, or suggestions about the site or content.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Submit the contact form and remain on the page while showing a success or error message
Click on the site logo or "Home" in the header while viewing Contact

access_methods.description: Accessed via the "Contact" link in the site footer
Linked from About or other pages inviting feedback

pages.name: Privacy Policy
pages.description: Informational page describing how the site handles data, privacy, and analytics for the single user.
primary_functions: Explain what data is stored locally (e.g., reading lists, collections, settings) and how it is used.
Describe any analytics or logging behavior relevant to this single-user setup.
Outline the users privacy rights and data retention practices.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a link to the Terms of Use from the Privacy Policy content
Click on the site logo or "Home" in the header while viewing Privacy Policy

access_methods.description: Accessed via the "Privacy Policy" link in the site footer

pages.name: Terms of Use
pages.description: Legal terms governing acceptable use of the site and its hacking-related content.
primary_functions: Define acceptable and prohibited uses of tutorials and hacking techniques presented on the blog.
Clarify liability, warranties, and responsibilities related to following the sites cybersecurity guidance.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a link to the Privacy Policy from the Terms of Use content
Click on the site logo or "Home" in the header while viewing Terms of Use

access_methods.description: Accessed via the "Terms of Use" link in the site footer

header_links.description: Homepage with featured cybersecurity content and global search
Beginner-friendly cybersecurity and Wi-Fi security articles
Threat intelligence, phishing, malware, and related topics
Security tools reviews, setup guides, and usage tips
Multi-part learning series such as Web App Penetration Testing

footer_links.description: Information about the blog and its author
Details on data and privacy practices
Legal terms and acceptable use of content
View and manage saved articles and collections
Configure personal preferences and notification topics
